title: Printer Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose Windows printer issues with spooler, driver, queue, and connectivity checks.
starter_prompt: Troubleshoot this printer problem. Start with read-only checks and ask before clearing queues or restarting services.

phases.prompt: Confirm printer name, USB/network connection, error message, affected apps, and whether other users can print.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Printers =='; Get-Printer | Select-Object Name,PrinterStatus,Default,DriverName,PortName,Shared | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Print queues =='; Get-PrintJob -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object PrinterName,ID,JobStatus,SubmittedTime,DocumentName | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Spooler service =='; Get-Service Spooler | Format-List Name,Status,StartType; Write-Output '== Drivers =='; Get-PrinterDriver | Select-Object Name,Manufacturer,DriverVersion | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Ports =='; Get-PrinterPort | Select-Object Name,PrinterHostAddress,PortNumber,Protocol | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Recent print events =='; Get-WinEvent -LogName Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational -MaxEvents 80 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object TimeCreated,Id,LevelDisplayName,Message | Format-List

phases.prompt: Collect read-only printer list, default printer, queue state, spooler service status, print event logs, drivers, and ports.
Identify whether issue is queue, spooler, driver, port, network, or application-specific. Recommend safest fix.
phases.completion: Likely cause and repair choice are documented.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Restart print spooler =='; Restart-Service Spooler -Force; Start-Sleep -Seconds 2; Get-Service Spooler | Format-List Name,Status,StartType; Write-Output 'Spooler restart complete. Queue clearing and test print are intentionally not automatic.'

phases.prompt: After approval/restore gate, perform only targeted repair: restart the print spooler. Do not clear queues or print a test page automatically.
phases.completion: Approved printer repair completed or skipped.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Printer state =='; Get-Printer | Select-Object Name,PrinterStatus,Default,DriverName,PortName | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Queue state =='; Get-PrintJob -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object PrinterName,ID,JobStatus,SubmittedTime,DocumentName | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Spooler =='; Get-Service Spooler | Format-List Name,Status,StartType

phases.prompt: Verify printer exists, spooler is running, queue is healthy, and port/driver status is readable. Do not print test page automatically unless requested.

Create a technician-readable printer troubleshooting report with issue, evidence, actions/tools used, repair result, verification result, logs/artifacts, cleanup status, residual risk, and any manual test-page/user action needed.
phases.completion: User has a printer troubleshooting report.

phases.prompt: Preserve logs and report; remove only workflow-owned temporary scratch files.

acceptance_criteria: Printer state and event logs were captured first.
Queue clear or service restart required approval.
Driver/port/spooler status is included in report.
No test page was printed unless user requested it.
